content.full_text:
**State of California  
GOVERNOR'S OFFICE  
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814**

March 8, 1991

Mr. Mark Morodomi  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: Donation of Honoraria

Dear Mr. Morodomi:

I am writing to confirm our conversation of March 7, 1991.

Under Political Reform Act provisions which became effective January 1, 1991, no elected official may accept any honorarium. However, an honorarium which is not used, is returned to the donor within 30 days, or donated to the general fund, as specified, is not an honorarium within the meaning of the statute.

I asked if an elected official could ask the donor of a proffered honorarium to send the funds to a charitable organization. You said such an arrangement would not violate the statute.

Sincerely,

Janice Rogers Brown  
Legal Affairs Secretary
